repositories.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Use before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies. Parallel subagent execution.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Use when implementation is complete and all tests pass. Guides completion via merge, PR, or cleanup.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
skills.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or questionable.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session. Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Random fixes waste time.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix. Write the test first, watch it fail, write minimal code to pass.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace. Creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection.

skills.name: using-superpowers
skills.description: Use when starting any conversation. Establishes how to find and use skills in the superpowers framework.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing. Evidence before assertions, before committing or creating PRs.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code. Write comprehensive implementation plans.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment. TDD applied to process documentation.
